primary_source: Alberta Electoral Boundaries Commission Final Report March 23 2026
notes: Audit's own finding; 1 UCP seat shift (67/22 majority vs 66/23 minority under 338 Apr 2026 polling; 35/51 vs 38/51 under 2023 actual vote)

primary_source: Alberta Electoral Boundaries Commission Final Report Oct 2017; Interim Report May 2017
notes: Both reports available as PDFs at elections.ab.ca; would require extracting per-ED populations and vote attribution for both interim and final; not acquired in this session

primary_source: Alberta Electoral Boundaries Commission Final Report 2010
notes: Predecessor cycle; 87-seat baseline established; full interim-to-final comparison not performed in this session

Federal Electoral Boundaries Commission publishes proposal and final reports per province. Not aggregated to national-level partisan-asymmetry measurement in this session; would require extracting per-riding 2021 vote totals for both proposal and final maps across all 10 provinces.

primary_source: Report of the Federal Electoral Boundaries Commission for Alberta Feb 2023
notes: Alberta federal commission proposal and final reports both public; requires crosswalk from 2021 polls to proposal boundaries AND to final boundaries; not built in this session

primary_source: BC Electoral Boundaries Commission Final Report April 2023
notes: Preliminary report April 2022; final report April 2023; 6-seat increase to 93 total. Per-riding vote crosswalk not acquired in this session.

primary_source: Saskatchewan Constituency Boundaries Commission Final Report April 2022
notes: Commission's interim report published 2021; final April 2022. Province has no provincial-level vote dataset matched to the 2022 proposed boundaries to the granularity the Alberta audit uses; RT equivalent not computable in this session without substantial additional scraping.

primary_source: Fewer Politicians Act SO 1996; Electoral Boundaries Readjustment Act 1996
notes: Commonly cited Canadian comparator for government override. Government reduced Ontario seats from 130 to 103 by legislative action after commission; no apples-to-apples partisan asymmetry comparable to Alberta 2026 minority-majority because there was only one final map.

primary_source: Commission de la representation electorale du Quebec Final Report 1992; Loi electorale amendment 1992
notes: Government amended specific ridings after commission report. Minor in scope vs Ontario 1996.

primary_source: BC Electoral Boundaries Commission Final Report 2008; Electoral Boundaries Commission Amendment Act 2008
notes: Government added 6 seats by legislative amendment after commission requested a two-seat expansion; BC 2008 is the closest Canadian precedent to Alberta 2026 in substantive scope.

primary_source: Manitoba Electoral Divisions Boundaries Commission Final Report 2018
Nova Scotia Electoral Boundaries Commission Final Report 2019
Commission on Electoral Boundaries and Representation Final Report 2023
